---
title: "SRKW-QERM514"
author: "Mollie Ball"
date: "2026-05-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


INFORMATION ABOUT THE DATASET SHOULD GO HERE:

```{r Loads in Data}
getwd()
# LOADS IN PHYLOSEQ AND OTHER RDATA OBJECTS FOR SRKW PROJECT
# includes reads that are at least 1% of diet in 4 or more samples
load("SRKW-diet-MAJOR-16SALL.Rdata")

```


WHY DO THEY NEED TO BE RELATIVE ABUNDANCE AND NOT ABSOLUTE ABUNDANCE?
```{r Calculates relative abundance}
# Calculates relative abundance of each species 
## Transforms NaN (0/0) to 0
ps.16s.major.rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.16s.major, function(x) {
  x_rel <- x / sum(x)
  x_rel[is.nan(x_rel)] <- 0
  return(x_rel)
})
```

I WANT TO LOOK AT THE PERCENT OF CHINOOK IN THE DIET ACROSS DIFFERENT PREDICTOR VARIABLES
SEASON
AGE CLASS
SEX
POD
YEAR

```{r Creates new df}
# Calculates relative abundance of each species 
## Transforms NaN (0/0) to 0
ps.16s.major.rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.16s.major, function(x) {
  x_rel <- x / sum(x)
  x_rel[is.nan(x_rel)] <- 0
  return(x_rel)
})
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r Shannon Diversity by Pod}
pr16s_data <- plot_richness(
  ps.16s, x = "Year",
  measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson"),
  color = "pod"
)$data

```


```{r Shannon Diversity by Month}
# Orders Months Correctly for Plotting
pr16s_data$Month <- factor(pr16s_data$Month, levels = c("Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sept", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"))

# Filters for Shannon Diversity
sh_data <- pr16s_data %>%
  filter(pr16s_data$variable == "Shannon")

p1 <- ggplot(sh_data, aes(Month, value)) +
  geom_point()

p1
```
Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
